title: Nested try-catch-finally Flow in Java
description: A flowchart illustrating the execution flow and exception handling when using nested try statements in Java.
relationships: Execution enters the Outer try block
If an exception occurs in the Inner try, it is handled by the matching Inner catch
The Inner finally block always executes after the Inner try/catch
If the Inner catch cannot handle the exception, it propagates to the Outer catch
The Outer finally block always executes after the Outer try/catch
Execution proceeds to the Next Statement only if all exceptions are handled or none occur
